steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that the authors describe how restorative sleep can be for young people. The correct quotation must mention both (1) sleep and (2) its restorative effect on the young.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '“Let us, dear reader remember the punishment of idle curiosity, as taught in the true and affecting history [named] Blue Beard; and, striving to be content with the facts in the case, seek not to lift the veil, which the sensibility of truelove, and feminine delicacy, have alike conspired to draw.”': About curiosity and Blue Beard — no mention of sleep. ✗
  • Choice B — '“Then let no one turn with too much [fussiness] from the simple story of the humble Elleanor, though it may contain few, or none, of the thrilling charms of poetry and passion.”': About the story itself, not sleep. ✗
  • Choice C — '“Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.”': "Slumbers" = sleep; "refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood" = restorative for the young. Both elements present. ✓
  • Choice D — '“Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great, and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.”': About home, not sleep. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
